name: Aush ( Noodles with Pulses, Meat and Yoghurt)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Canned kidney beans with liquid
recipeInstructions:
Serves: 6
Cooking time: 1 to 1-1/4 hours
1. Sift the flour and salt into a bowl. Add water gradually and mix until it forms a firm dough. If needed, add more flour.
2. Divide the dough into two balls and wrap in plastic. Let rest for 30 minutes.
3. On a floured board, roll out each ball of dough thinly. Cut into 1/4-inch strips (5 mm). Place noodles on a floured cloth and dust with flour. Let dry for about 30 minutes.
4. Wash split peas well and place in a pan with 1-1/2 cups cold water. Bring to boil and cook gently for 30 minutes or until tender. Add red beans and liquid, keeping warm.
5. In a large pot, bring 8 cups water to a boil. Add salt, oil, and noodles gradually, stirring after each addition. Return to boil and cook uncovered for 5 minutes. Add spinach and cook for another 5 minutes. Drain in a colander and return to the pot. Add split peas with their liquid and bean mixture, tossing lightly. Keep hot over low heat.
6. In a frying pan, heat oil. Fry onion until soft and add ground meat. Stir until juices evaporate and meat browns lightly. Add salt, pepper, tomato puree, and water. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes, then remove cover and let moisture evaporate. The sauce should be oily.
7. Combine chakah ingredients (yogurt, mint, chili pepper, coriander, and salt). Mix well with noodles and toss thoroughly. Serve in deep plates.
Place the noodle mixture in a deep dish and top with keema. Stir at the table before serving.
